title: No Cost EMI, They Said
scenario: A festival sale offers a premium ₹60,000 laptop on a 12-month 'no cost EMI' plan. Your current laptop works fine for your job. Do you swipe for it?

choices.label: Buy it on a 12-month EMI
choices.lesson: EMIs make expensive wants feel affordable by hiding the full cost. Several 'tiny' EMIs together can quietly trap a big share of your income.

choices.label: Keep my current laptop
choices.lesson: Borrowing for wants (not needs) mortgages your future income. The cheapest gadget is often the one you already own and don't replace.

concept: Consumer debt, EMIs & cost of borrowing
